summary: A 64-year-old with established ASCVD (prior MI 18 months ago) on maximally tolerated atorvastatin 80 mg has an LDL of 92 mg/dL and asks about ezetimibe as an add-on. The held-out answer must reflect the AHA/ACC 2018 cholesterol guideline recommendation of an LDL goal <70 mg/dL (or <55 mg/dL for very-high-risk per ESC 2019 / 2022 update considerations the patient may have read about), the IMPROVE-IT trial finding (modest absolute risk reduction over simvastatin alone), and ezetimibe's strong tolerability/safety profile.

messages.content: I had a heart attack about a year and a half ago. I'm on atorvastatin 80 mg, my LDL last month was 92. My cardiologist mentioned adding ezetimibe. Is that worth it, or am I already where I need to be?

do_NOT: claim LDL 92 is 'at goal' for someone with established ASCVD on max-tolerated statin
describe ezetimibe as more potent than a high-intensity statin
issue prescriptive directives without acknowledging shared decision making

edges_required_for_correct_answer: patient:established_ascvd_post_mi --aha_acc_2018--> target:ldl_lt_70 (very-high-risk subset; <55 per ESC update)

expected_sources_referenced: AHA/ACC/multisociety 2018 Cholesterol Guideline — Grundy et al., J Am Coll Cardiol 2019
IMPROVE-IT trial — Cannon et al., NEJM 2015
ESC/EAS 2019 dyslipidaemia guideline (Mach et al., Eur Heart J 2020) and 2021 CV-prevention update — for very-high-risk LDL <55 framing
